cards.front: What happens when you improve 1% each day for a year?
cards.back: You become approximately 37 times better. Small improvements compound into remarkable results over time.

cards.front: What is the 'valley of disappointment' in habit building?
cards.back: The period when you're doing the work but not yet seeing visible results — because habits haven't yet reached the compounding breakthrough point. Most people give up here.

cards.front: What is 'deep work' and why is it valuable?
cards.back: Deep work is focused, distraction-free effort on cognitively demanding tasks. It's valuable because it's becoming rare (due to distraction culture) while the knowledge economy increasingly rewards complex cognitive output.
